PR surface: Source +24, Tests +45. Total +69 across 7 files.

target.title: fix(webchat): keep media loads pinned to bottom
target.body:
Closes #96593.

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where WebChat users at the bottom of a conversation could lose the pinned-to-latest position when assistant audio or video attachments finished loading metadata and changed layout height after the message was rendered.

## Why This Change Was Made

The media metadata event now routes through the existing chat scroll scheduler, so the same near-bottom and intentional-scroll-up guards handle delayed audio/video layout changes instead of adding a separate scroll path.

## User Impact

Assistant media replies stay anchored to the newest message when the user is already at the bottom, while users who have intentionally scrolled up are not pulled back down.

## Evidence

Manual UI validation: macOS with headless Chromium, using Vite-served Control UI modules from this branch. Rendering an assistant MEDIA audio/video reply through the real renderMessageGroup and scheduleChatScroll path kept audio metadata load pinned with bottomDistance=0; after an intentional user scroll-up, video metadata left scrollTop unchanged at 319 and set newMessagesBelow=true.

Focused coverage passed for the chat scroll path, chat view forwarding, and grouped media metadata callback wiring.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WebChat assistant MEDIA audio/video layout changes after metadata load keep the transcript pinned when the user is near bottom, without overriding an intentional scroll-up.
Real environment tested: macOS, Chromium via Playwright, Vite-served Control UI modules from this branch.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rendered an assistant MEDIA audio/video reply, expanded the audio/video controls before firing loadedmetadata, and observed the real scroll scheduler behavior.
Evidence after fix: audio metadata left bottomDistance=0; video metadata after user scroll-up left scrollTop unchanged at 319 and set newMessagesBelow=true.
Observed result after fix: media loads stayed pinned to the newest message when appropriate, and user scroll-up remained respected.
What was not tested: A live gateway-backed Safari session.
